Gaurab
Home Projects CV

Hi, I’m Gaurab.

My hometown is Kathmandu, Nepal. I graduated in Fall 2021 from Oberlin College with a degree in Computer Science (High Honors) and Mathematics. I then earned my M.S. in Computer Science (Machine Learning concentration) from George Mason University. Currently, I am continuing my Ph.D. in Computer Science at Virginia Tech's Institute for Advanced Computing under the advisement of Dr. Sanmay Das.

As a researcher in AI and machine learning, my interests span game theory and its applications in the allocation of scarce societal resources. I am particularly passionate about exploring how AI can address issues of distributive justice, and I remain committed to investigating the implications of various policies and mechanisms in this context.

Current focus
Discretion as a resource in dynamic allocation
Methods
Interpretable ML, Dynamic Models, Simulation
Now
Building Tools + Writing Papers + Dissertation

News

  • 2026-03-02
    Accepted: Fixed Points and Stochastic Meritocracies
    Our paper Fixed Points and Stochastic Meritocracies: A Long-Term Perspective was accepted to FAccT 2026.
  • 2026-02-20
    Preprint: Budgeting Discretion
    New preprint on modeling discretion as a budgeted resource available online .
